steps.title: Spot the experimental limitation
steps.content: The researchers explicitly state that the shielding didn't tell them how much loss was “due specifically to excluding honeybees.” This signals that something beyond just honeybees was affected by the shielding.

steps.title: Identify the confounding factor
steps.content:
C directly proves the limitation: squash bees also pollinate cucumbers, and the shielding blocked them too.

The 40-90% loss could therefore stem from excluding multiple pollinators, not honeybees alone, which is exactly why the researchers cannot isolate honeybee effects.
  • A rules out sunlight as a confound, but it does not show that non-honeybee cucumber pollinators were also blocked
  • B discusses a different crop
  • D notes relative usage rather than experimental limitations

That matches Choice C — Other insects like squash bees also pollinate cucumber plants, and the shielding in the first phase of the study blocked them as well..
